title: BenefitsApplication
description: A resident's application for a public-benefit program (SNAP, Cash Assistance, Medicaid, and others) — the everyday HRA transaction. NET-NEW: there is NO public API and NO Open Data twin for an individual application or case; today residents apply and check case status only inside the ACCESS HRA portal (a069-access.nyc.gov/accesshra, a React SPA behind Akamai) or in person at a Benefits Access / SNAP Center. This schema serves both the read surface (list/get a resident's own applications and their status) and the write surface (submit a benefits application).

applicationId.description: Server-assigned application / case number (ACCESS HRA). Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Case lifecycle status. Set by HRA, not the applicant.

additionalPrograms.description: Other programs applied for in the same submission (ACCESS HRA lets a resident apply for several at once).

emergency.description: Flag an immediate need (no food, eviction, no heat) — routes to expedited SNAP / emergency assistance handling.

eligibilityScreeningRef.description: Optional reference to a prior BenefitsEligibility screening result.

documents.description: Supporting documents uploaded with the application (ACCESS HRA document upload).

type.description: Document type (ID, proof of income, lease, etc.).

preferredCenter.description: Preferred Benefits Access / SNAP Center for any in-person step.
